violations.rule: Back brevity / no 'and' joining two facts
violations.detail: Back is "Faster per update, and the noise helps escape shallow local minima" — two distinct facts joined by 'and'. Should be split into two cards: one for speed advantage, one for noise benefit.

Cards 3 and 4 form a sacrifice/gain mirror pair. Learning rate has two extremes with two failure modes; knowing 'too large → overshoots/diverges' strongly hints 'too small → slow convergence' (and vice versa), since these are the only two obvious failure modes of a scalar hyperparameter. The self-check claimed the failure modes are different enough, but the SKILL.md requires merging or dropping when knowing one substantially narrows the other. Should merge into a single multi-cloze or keep only the less obvious one.

The self-check claims ~3 definitional, ~3 why/how, ~2 what-happens-if, but cards 3, 4 are 'what happens if' (application), cards 6, 7 are why/how reasoning, card 8 is reasoning, cards 1, 2, 5 are definitional/factual. That gives 3/8 = 37.5% definitional, which is within range, so this is borderline acceptable. Minor concern only.

score_breakdown.card_quality: Generally strong — atomic fronts, mostly brief backs, good context, no yes/no questions, no example-asking, proper cloze usage
score_breakdown.card_selection: Good topic coverage, no trivia/orphans/label-only cards, content-based, meets minimum cluster size
score_breakdown.deductions: -10 for card 6 back brevity violation (clear 'and' joining two facts), -8 for cards 3&4 mirror-deducible pair
